description: A vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic from USCIS using a dramatic red-to-blue duotone gradient overlay on a photograph of a street arrest. The scene shows, from behind, a tactical officer at left wearing a ballcap, a face covering/gaiter, and a vest with patches reading 'POLICE' and 'VIOLENT GANG TASK FORCE'; he escorts a handcuffed man (wrists cuffed behind back) who wears a patterned/baroque-print jacket and camouflage pants, walking along a residential street lined with brick apartment buildings. The left side of the image glows red and the upper-right is tinted blue, an evocative, cinematic color treatment. A solid dark-blue rectangle in the lower-left contains the slogan in bold white serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.' This is the recurring DHS/USCIS enforcement-branding template. The messaging technique pairs a gang-task-force arrest with the 'Making America Safe Again' (MAGA-echoing) slogan to frame immigration enforcement as crime-fighting and public protection. Target audience is the general public and Trump's base.
